title: SkillSpec — composer artifact (C-series)
description: The decision object composer emits. Six-vendor-intersection JSON: object root, all required, no min* constraints, snake_case, English. Tri-state (filled / explicit-unknown / explicit-dispute) and build-vs-reject consistency are enforced by the L0 script validate_spec, NOT by this schema. Anchors: C2 fifteen-field object, C7 reject branch.

target.description: skill name or path this spec is for

verdict.description: C7: 'reject' (not-a-skill) is a legal, successful output

rejection.description: Populated when verdict=reject (C7 requires a VERIFIED alternative, not a verbal dismissal). When verdict=build, still emit this object with empty-string fields — it is only enforced on reject; it may also carry the reject-first triage's alternative-run evidence.

alternative.description: bare model / one prompt / existing skill that already does it

alternative_evidence.description: record of actually running the task the alternative way (C7: no verbal reject)

build_spec.description: The C2 fifteen-field decision object. When verdict=reject this may be skeletal (script relaxes the completeness check).

task.description: C1 narrative: who, at what moment, blocked by what, cost of failure

users.description: who uses it, expertise, failure tolerance

success.description: C5 objective/subjective fork declared per dimension

failure_cost.description: failure modes + each cost, incl. unacceptable failures; feeds E9 stop strictness

rejected.description: considered-and-rejected approaches + reasons. Empty = strong un-thought signal (gate spot-checks).

unknowns.description: explicit unknowns, EACH with a discovery plan (C2). A dimension is legally blank only if it appears here.

disputes.description: explicit disputes; candidates kept, not force-unified (C2)

stop.description: C2: BOTH a done-criterion AND an abandon-criterion

narratives.description: C1/C4: >=2 usage narratives, >=1 adversarial/out-of-bounds. Script enforces the counts + adversarial flag.

scenario.description: concrete story with real detail (file paths, values); each 'skill should do X' maps back to a spec field

author.description: C4 recommends a non-author subagent for the adversarial one

faq.description: C3 six forced-rebuttal questions, each with a substantive answer or a pointer into unknowns. Minimum set, may grow not shrink.

trigger_tests.description: C6: positives, near-miss negatives (shared keyword, wrong intent), nearest-neighbor boundary sentences. Each case carries an expected_frequency tier.

why_not.description: shares keywords but should NOT trigger
